top_categories.workout_context: Granfondo Gottardo's paved Alpine roads with historic cobblestone forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 71 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Granfondo Gottardo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before August.
10,656ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
At Granfondo Gottardo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
150ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
10,656ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Granfondo Gottardo. Train it until it feels boring.
Low-cadence force work for 10,656ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
71 miles on paved Alpine roads with historic cobblestone burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Granfondo Gottardo, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Base fitness for Granfondo Gottardo. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on paved Alpine roads with historic cobblestone. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Granfondo Gottardo’s 71 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Granfondo Gottardo with 10,656ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%. Three temperature swings from valley to summit — 15-20C differentials on each pass.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Granfondo Gottardo requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on paved alpine roads with historic cobblestone section to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Gravel Specific, Race Simulation, and VO2max to prepare you for 71 miles of paved alpine roads with historic cobblestone section in Ambri, Ticino, Switzerland.
